summary: "DnnImageHandlers > Enhance IPCount mechanism to avoid file locking"
description: """The dnnImageHandler IPCount mechanism to prevent DoS attack write files under AppData. In 8.0.0 regression we have found some locking issues and this locking can be more aggressive in web farm scenario (see related issue DNN-8086).    The goal of this improvement is change the mechanism to use memory (i.e.: cache) or database to provide a more roboust solution.    *Important*  Caching should be the first option as we want have a very high performance mechanism. Database would imply a considerable number of read/writes that could hit performance. In that case we should ensure that the solution does not impact the site overall performance.  If we use a system based on cache we should consider web farm scenario.   """

summary: "Implement Moniker API for Service Framework "
description: """A moniker is a simple name provided for an objects that simplifies identifying specific object instances.  This concept was first used in Microsoft Object Linking and Embedding (OLE), and the Component Object Model (COM).      The DNN Services Framework was first introduced in DNN 6.2.  When creating web services with the Service Framework, it is common to require callers to provide the ModuleId and TabId as part of the web request header.  This requirement complicates the programming model since service endpoints become tied to their specific location in a website.  Modules are able to easily retrieve the ModuleId and TabId from the service framework as part of the initial module rendering.  However, this mechanism is not available for remote callers, where the UI rendering is not directly controlled by DNN.    In order to encapsulate this context data and to simplify the programming model for web services, we should introduce the concept of a moniker.  The DNN service moniker will provide a mechanism for mapping a simple name to a specific module instance.  This moniker can then be passed to a web service in place of the ModuleId, TabId or any other context data specific to a module.    The moniker should be implemented as an additional system TabModule setting. By default, the moniker will be composed of the page name, the module definition friendly name and the TabModuleId separated by /.  When composing the default moniker, all spaces should be replaced with dashes.  For example, if the Social Groups module was on the “Community” page with TabModuleId of 100, the default moniker would be “Community/Social-Groups/100”.      The default moniker implementation provides a human friendly method to identify the page and module being addressed and also a unique element to differentiate between multiple modules on the same page.  The core platform should expose a service endpoint that allows an application to query for monikers related to a specific module type that the current user has permissions to view.    When calling service endpoints that need a specific module context, the moniker will be included as part of the service call.  The service can then use the moniker to easily lookup any context for the module instance associated with the moniker.    *Developer Notes:*  The purpose of this step is to help the back-end to create the DNN context for the web API request. Therefore, it is not tied to any authentication mechanism (like JWT, Basic, Forms, etc.)    Injecting the moniker in the query part of the API request requires modifying all existing API’s that need to use this moniker  but is not feasible and requires making changes to existing core (which we want to completely avoid). What we need is a non-invasive method. One way in which this can be implemented is by adding a new vendor specific header in the request as an alternate way to using the {{TabID}} and {{ModuleID}} that are in use today. Assume we are going to use {{X-DNN-MONIKER}} for this purpose. An example of a full header would be:  {{X-DNN-MONIKER: John’s Blog}}    Before being able to use this feature, there is a need for an API that returns the various monikers associated with a specific module. Let’s say in an EVOQ Engage site if we need to find all the monikers that use the discussions module, say discussions (i.e., all tabs/pages that have the discussions module in them), we will send an API request that returns all of these monikers associated with discussion modules placed on various pages. This call may return zero, one, or more monikers.    There must be a consideration for user rights in every data returned from the API; no private data should be revealed to an un-authorized user."""
comments: "there is no need for tabID or moduledefinitionid, both are identified by TabModuleID.  The moniker should be able to identify the module to direct the request to the appropriate module." summary: "Allow Module to bump cdv version"
description: """We recently ran into a problem where specific module have to create its own version of cdv concept to force a client side refresh of any css and js after upgrading.    In this proposal we should study how to resolve this issue at platform level with the following possible approaches:  # add a new tag in the module manifest a new flag to force cdv increment after module upgrade  ## pro: we reuse the same cdv mechanism provided in the platform  ## cons: we are refreshing the entire static assets cache of the site just for a specific module  NOTE: we should ensure adding a new tag in the manifest will be backward compatible if module vendor want to allow its module to install in previous platform version. In those the tag will be just ingnored.  # consider the creation of a new mechanism at module level (i.e. mcdv) provided by the platform that will automatically append this new parameter and force specific module assets client cache invalidation  ## pro: we will refresh only the module specific assets (not the entire site)  ## cons: if modules are using the same js/css we are forcing the client to down it twice    We will need to consider the two solutions."""

summary: "Ability to upload or copy-paste a manifest to create a module"
description: """For developers it's important to be able to hook up a new module in development as quickly and painlessly as possible. Once familiar with DNN we know how to create a manifest. Currently the only way to create a module from a manifest is through a convoluted way to load it from disk. This process is unnecessarily complex and rigid (it needed to be adapted to cater for MVC modules and now the Personabar modules would need yet another change). I propose to offer either a simple file upload or a text area to paste/test the manifest. IMHO we can even simply replace the current solution with this."""

summary: "Improve performance of Type Scanning"
description: """Some features require all the types to be scanned on startup, in order to determine if a type implements a specific interface.  This is currently done for Service Route Mapping and the Module Injection filters.    While removing unnecessary assemblies from the bin will improve this, there are a number of necessary assemblies (that we can't remove) that we know do not have any types.    Examples are the Telerik assemblies and the Microsoft assemblies.    We should therefore optimize the scanning of the assemblies so that these are explicitly ignored.    Secondly, we scan the types independently for each interface check - it might be possible to optimize this and scan once for both interfaces.  """
comments: "I did some modifications to the AssemblyLocator class to improve the Type Scanning logic.    Currently this class returns ""ALL"" assemblies and in my default install this consumed nearly 10% of the first page cost, as it returned 242 (121 x 2 executions) assemblies which caused 88,000 types to be scanned.     I modified the scan by eliminating known assemblies in two ways -   # if the assembly starts with Microsoft, System, Telerik or other common known namesapces it was ignored.  # if the assembly was a specific known assembly that had no Service Routes or Module Injection types it was ignored    This reduced the number of assemblies scanned to 80 (2 x 40) and the number of types scanned to just under 14,000, thus reducing the cost to 0.34% of the first page." [SEP] "Cherry-picked code to a new branch (bug/dnn-7458) based on latest development branch, and submitted as a new PR, which was merged into the latest development.    https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/851    " [SEP] "What QA should t

summary: "upgrade 51 degrees to 3.2.3.2"
description: """From Pull Request: https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/621    •We have performed load testing and published the results: https://51degrees.com/blog/ArtMID/1641/ArticleID/368/Memory-and-Performance-Impact-of-51Degrees   •We have added multiple unit tests: https://github.com/51Degrees/.NET-Device-Detection/tree/master/UnitTests   •We have been running the updated version of the DLL on our own website (also powered by DNN) for quite some time now.    The design focus of the release is to integrate DNN efficiently with 51Degrees. Improvements include reducing memory consumption when the data file used directly from the disk, and to make use of 51Degrees performance improvements.    List of changes:  •When device detection is disabled the Client Capabilities Provider no longer forces it to start.  •All possible properties and values are no longer stored in a SortedList. Only required properties and values are loaded when needed and cached.  •Embedded data has been removed from the assembly and now must be provided from the App_Data folder.  •.NET 3.5 is no longer supported in order to use memory mapped files and simplify overriding default browser capabilities.  •In stream mode entity data properties that can allocate large arrays only initialise these arrays when needed.  •Caches used with stream operation are now fixed memory size and serviced via the thread pool.  •Automatic update processes uses temporary files rather than main memory to verify integrity of updated files prior to using them.  •Temporary files are now created in the App_Data/51Degrees folder of the web application rather than a UNC path or the master data file folder.  •Values associated with Profiles are now retrieved using a more efficient algorithm.  •DataSet.Properties collection now has a string accesser to make retrieving properties by name simpler.  •Memory mode no longer initialises the entire dataset. Startup time is now significantly improved.  •Memory mode uses less memory than prior versions.  •Supports 51Degrees V3.2 data format using approximately 25% smaller file sizes.  """
comments: "backported to 7.4.2" [SEP] "additional changes checked in directly to fix time-sensitive build issue:  1. update 51degrees test project to use v32 version of 51degrees dat file, and 3.2.3.2 version of dll    2. commented out test that was failed where TC did not respect the ignore directive.    3. removed  FiftyOneClientCapabilityProvider_GetClientCapabilityById_ThrowsException_For_NonExisting_ClientCapabilityId test from test project (as 51degrees logic no longer returns an exception but returns the useragent - this is necessary to support disabling 51degrees without breaking 3rd party integrations)    Note: these were all done in https://dnntracker.atlassian.net/browse/DNN-7433 and then cherrypicked over to the development branch to ensure they were in 8.0.0" [SEP] "Also removed FiftyOne.Foundation.dll from Evoq's repository. This was a copy of the same DLL checked-in into Evoq as was in Platform. Now Evoq simply points to file from Platform. " [SEP] "I profiled the memory use of 51D in both {{Platform 8.

It also allows for a personal denial of use attack on someone (just create an account on a site with their email address and they are blocked out).   Closing this issue."

summary: "GetModulesByDefinition method should use caching for faster performance"
description: """The ModuleController.GetModulesByDefinition() method should perform a cache lookup to avoid querying the database like its counterpart GetModuleByDefinition() method is already doing. The GetModuleByDefinition is limited to returning the first arbitrary module only, which is less useful than the GetModulesByDefinition that returns the full information.    Any relatively complex module with more than one module definition that requires figuring out if other supporting modules are added to the site will call this method, often for as many module definitions it has leading to multiple database queries per page. For example, a shopping cart module has many cross-functional links on the shopping page that needs to figure out if the category, product list, product detail, cart modules are present will query the database repeatedly leading to poor performance.    Unfortunately, this method call cannot be cached by the calling application because it has no way to properly invalidate the cache (when page is deleted, module removed from page, etc.) that only the core library can know.    The cache implementation should be quite easy to implement simply by following/reusing the similar cache code in the GetModuleByDefinition method.    Thanks,"""
comments: "there is a difficulty in cache dependency, as any update to a moduledefinition and module needs to clear this cache as well"

summary: "DCC API - Dynamic Content Item is tied to Module Id"
description: """In the current architecture the Dynamic Content Item instance is tied to a specific module. The Content Item table stores the ModuleId and the TabId:  !DynamicContentItemTiedToModule.png!    Basically the content item is who says: """"I belong to the module"""" when we should invert the relationship and the module should say: """"I in charge to render the content item"""".  We want the content item decoupled of the viewer so we potencially can share and integrate this content outside the dnn system or the viewer module.    The Viewer makes use of this information to know which Content Item is associated with him:      *Solution proposal*:  We should store the Content Item with no TabId or ModuleId associated and store in the Module Setting instead the Content Item Id of the Dynamic Content.  Currently the Module already use Module Setting to know which content type is used and which Edit and View template is set:  !DynamicViewerModuleSettings.png!    Note: we could eventually replace the Content Type Setting with Content Item Id.  * DCC_ContentItemId      *Important Notes*    * *Note for Dev*: we need to review the scenario where the Module is deleted. Probably for this first version we want also be able to remove the Content Item from the system as well. Need to confirmed with PM.    * *Note for QA*: this task is more API level and does not imply changes in funcionality. Just how the relationship between the content item and the module is defined."""
comments: "After discuss this task with [~accountid:<USER>, we may want for this first version still mantain the value of ModuleId and TabId in the Content Item table, even if this information won't be used for now.  However we should need to change the way the Viewer load the Content Item as commented in the jira." [SEP] "ModuleId and TabId are not no longer stored in the Content Item. The Viewer module now stores Module Setting to point to the Content Item Id (DCC_ContentItemId). I have mantained the Module Settings DCC_ContentType as needed for the initial module setup.    *DCC Module Setting after edited an Employee Content Item*: note that the module point to the Content Item (it is no longer the opposite, Content Item pointing to the Module)  !DCCViewerModuleSettings.png!    *Content Item table*: note that we do no have longer a relationship to the module from the Content Item. The DCC Viewer or any future DCC Visualizer shoudn't be stored as part of the Content Item.  !ContentItemTable.png!    *Note for QA:* as

summary: "Separate security checks fro SA & PB security in a common DLL"
description: """Currently there are two components that check for security issues in a DNN site. These are:  - the stand alone Security Analyzer  - the Persona Bar security extension    When issuing a security fix and updating one but not the other, there could be some confusion created for the customers where one of these checkers says an issue is fixed while the other says it is not.    To avoid this situation, we need to create a common DLL for the checks that can be used by both and any update in this library will be reflected in both of them. Also we need to modify both components mentioned above to rely on the common DLL to report any security issue.  """

summary: "Allow Override of prefixing Service to Username for OAuth providers."
description: """The fix for DNN-4133 was to add a prefix for the service to the beginning of the username. This broke our custom OAuth provider and I believe that we should have the ability to override whether we want to prefix the service to the Username in OAuth providers.  In addition to allowing that overriding, this also implements the option to automatically associate logins to existing users, based on email.    Testing of this API change will require a custom OAuth provider implementation which sets the new {{PrefixServiceToUserName}} and {{AutoMatchExistingUsers}} properties in that provider's implementation of {{OAuthClientBase}} to {{false}} and {{true}}, respectively.  That custom provider may be an existing provider (e.g. Facebook), tweaked to override those two properties.    When configured as mentioned above, the username of users who log in via that provider should be the user's email (as provided by the OAuth provider).  Additionally, if a user with that email address as their username already exists, that OAuth login would be associated with the existing account, rather than trying to create a new account."""

Conversation This initial conversion effort is only focused on conversion to a module and shall not include conversion to a MVC or SPA module.  h2. Confirmation """

summary: "Make Persona Bar UserSetting free of Evoq parameter in platform and extensible"
description: """https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.AdminExperience.Library/blob/development/src/Dnn.PersonaBar.Library/DTO/UserSettings.cs    Persona Bar user settings is a mechanism to store server side the user preference for the persona bar consume then in the UI.     These settings are used to store the latest panel open, if the panel was open, the latest query for analytics, etc… So the next time the user comes to the Persona bar will find the same setup, its preferences in place.    *Issue*  # The current implementation makes use of a Fixed Schema Entity Object (not extensible)  # The current implementation expose settings used only in Evoq  ** Some of these properties make sense for a product independent Persona Bar (i.e.: Expand PersonaBar, Active Path, Active Identifier), but other are fixed to feature in Evoq (i.e.: endDate, comparativeTerm, period, legends, expandTasks).    The goal of this improvement is remove any Evoq specific parameters and make the system extensible so other panels could take the benefit of this mechanism (starting with Evoq params).    *Note for DEV*: we have to consider if the new system is backward compatible, in case some third party could potencially using the old settings (however, I do not think will be the case as these are fixed and used already in specific area).  """

summary: "Move Liquid Content from Microservice to Platform"
description: """We want to move Liquid Content from the Evoq Microservice into modules and extensions within DNN Platform.      Important items to consider:  # Content Library, Content Types, Visualizers and API Keys need to be ported.    # Visualizer module needs to be ported.    # Search Integration  # Database structure/storage     Features not being migrated:  # Content Library -> Facebook, Twitter and LinkedIn Publishing      *Engineering Considerations*  # We need to have proper separation of concerns between web API, business layer and repository layers  # Where possible we should .Net Standard 2.0 class libraries  # Where possible remove dependencies of DNN Dlls  # Must support DNN's native:  ## Permission  ## Localization  ## Workflow   ## Search  # The above systems should be integrated using Dependency Injection  # Must use SQL Server for data storage, but the repository layer should be abstractable, where other data storage (e.g. MongoDB) can be used. Current Liquid Content uses DocumentDB which is Azure only service  ## We may require newer version of SQL Server, e.g. 2016 (especially if we want to take advantage of Json data types)   # Have source code in a new repository outside of Dnn.Platform. Our goal is to keep things as decoupled as possible - continue to slim down Platform, and certainly not more.  ## This should be Library type extension.   ## In theory, one could choose not to install Structured Content on DNN.  ## Any module taking advantage of SC must specify dependency on this library.  # Should be scalable, must support 1M+ content items with ease (of course depends on hardware). Solution should linearly scale with more hardware resources.     """

summary: "Conditionally include SPA script/css token support for settings"
description: """In a DNN 7 version of my module, I was able to expose a setting to my users to allow them to choose whether or not they would use debug, CDN, or local versions of script files.  With the current SPA implementation, we're unable to do any of that.  Our users have no choice in the matter.      We need a way through extensibility to tell the DNN rendering engine when/if to include a CSS/JS file and what version to include.      An example use case would be my module requiring Bootstrap.  However, if the site theme already has it, then I don't need to include it.  The site administrator should be able to make a decision here.  Maybe on the page with my module, they include a skin without the bootstrap dependencies and prefer I load them instead, or vice versa.  With the current implementation, there's nothing we can do either way.  It's currently a very technical decision instead."""

summary: "DCC - Sync Dynamic Content Item Json to Content Type Definition"
description: """Dynamic Content Creator wants to be a very flexible and dynamic content creator system. On Content Type already in use (we have content item already created in the system for a specific content type) we do not want restrict the content type definition and the system need to allow add, rename or delete a fields.    Currently the system is managing that (see DNN-7791) but when the Content Type fields change (add a new field, remove it) the content item previusly created with the old definition still keep the json with the old data. This impact also search that still is indexing this old information.    This task is to evaluate and consider some strategy to keep in sync the json stored in the dynamic content item json and the content type definition.    The goal of this task will consist in propose 2 or more strategies and evaluating for each of those pro and cons.                                """

summary: "Pages > Link-URL: Quit adding http:// if URL starts with ""#"""
description: """When building """"One Page Design"""" sites with DNN (quite popular in times of tables and mobile devices), it's a common task to point your page-URLs (=menu items) to text anchors (ex. """"#section1"""", """"#section2"""",..) inside the current page.    The URL-picker component in the Page > Link-URL dialog keeps adding a """"http://"""" to the beginning of the """"URL"""". This behavior forces the user to pick an absolute path to make the link work in the generated menu (""""http://www.myurl.com/#section1""""). If you only enter a relative """"#section1"""" as """"URL"""", the component will render the link as """"http://#section1"""", which will lead to a broken link.    In scenarios where there are multiple site-aliases, this turns out to be a little annoying, since it forces the user to switch to the """"primary"""" domain (or the one defined in the menu), as soon as he clicks on one of the menu items (=text anchors in this scenario).    Solution: Make the URL-dialog of the Link-Component a little less strict: Let the user add text-anchors (""""#section1"""") without 'automatically' adding a 'http://' in front of the URL, at least, if the """"URL"""" starts with """"#"""".    What do you think?"""
comments: "Batch update of issues that won't be addressed due to change in functionality from version 9.0"

summary: "Normalize userportal for superusers"
description: """While working on DNN-10425, we noticed performance issues due to missing normalization of Superusers. While each other user has at least one userportal record, superusers sometimes don't have userportal records or not for all portals. This requires a left join from UserPortal table to Users table, which is not useful for performance and a number of other isses, like missing UP properties.    The best solution would be creating userportal records for each superuser in any portal, which need to be completed a) when a superuser is created or b) a new portal is added. the superusers might also be added to RegisteredUsers Role, if they should appear in role member list.  If a user is turned into a superuser or back, no records will need to be adjusted anymore.    I am offering to provide the necessary SQL code, if this ticket gets scheduled for a release.  """

summary: "Folders Loading Performance Enhancement"
description: """This task aims at enhancing folders loading performance for sites with large amount of folders."""
comments: "From my experience, one of the biggest source of performance issues are user folders and its permissions on sites with large user base (like www.dnnsoftware.com).  My suggestions:  # create user folders not before a user tries to upload the first file.  # add an option to fake folderpermissions for user folders. Currently, there are 5 folderpermission records created per user folder -  which are usually never modified by the admin. We may simply take permissions of /users folder and add all permissions for the folder owner virtually in DNN API  Both suggestions should improve folder performance on large sites dramatically.   " [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER>, thanks for the suggestions. Actually, the first one is already in place for a while now, and the second is a good candidate for consideration. I'm working on a different approach now where I load the permissions for a specific folder only instead of all the permissions at once which will increase performance greatly for such sites." summary: "Site Import / Export"
description: """Content Staging is an Enterprise CMS feature that DNN is currently missing. To potentially get to content staging, we're starting with whole-site Import / Export. This will be a stand alone module in Persona Bar > Settings. This may require iPortable v2 and microservices multi-tenant capability. The target content for export is that generated as part of the CMS. Modules that are predominately site visitor/user driven content typically won't take part in this process (Engage modules like Answers, Discussions, Ideas, etc.).     *PERMISSION*  Admin and Host user will have access to this functionality, however Admin will have access only to their own site.    *WHAT ARE WE EXPORTING*  Existing:  * Content  * Files  * Roles  * Profile Properties  * Pages (pick list with option to select/de-select all with a single click)    New:  * Users (each user's membership information: username, password, email, )  * * User profile property values (if users are selected AND profile properties)  * Extension packages (No UI)  ** Modules/Providers/Themes/Language Packs  ** Folder permissions  * HTML Provider Configuration (No UI)  * Vocabularies  * URL's and redirects (No UI)  ** Any AUM settings (site level), any page level redirects  * Evoq page templates (No UI)  * Evoq Connector Data (most of this is probably under portal settings)  * All Portal Settings data (any property from the table should automatically)  ** Anything related to portal alias data will *not* be included  * All Page Settings not currently included, per page  ** This should include any page header, scripts, etc. seen via the page settings UI    *NOT IN SCOPE*  * Page version history  * Asset version history  * Transfer files to production  * Direct site-to-site connection  * Continuous integration  * Analytics data   * Workflow   * Community Module Content (Engage module content, for example)  * Mechanics Activity, Scoring Action or Privilege point data  * Portal Alias data    *EXPORT / IMPORT STEPS*  * Export: The export wizard will involve most of the decision points, in order to produce a final package that is universally accepted and standardized for DNN sites.  * Import: Import should involve less decisions, run without exception, and be a very painless process    *TESTING NOTES*  Upon Export, a .zip package should be created that installs into any other DNN site with similar versions.  """

summary: "Conditionally include SPA script/css token support for browsers"
description: """A critical thing to support when building any website is how to support Internet Explorer and sometimes other browsers too.  Unfortunately, there's currently no way for us to indicate to DNN when to include certain tokens, and when not to.  We are forced to always include all scripts/CSS regardless to the use case.      At a minimum, we should be able to indicate which web browser(s)/version(s) that a script needs to be included for.  For example IE shims.  Ideally, we should re-purpose a common standard when doing this, like with how we do it elsewhere with conditional HTML.    http://www.quirksmode.org/css/condcom.html"""

summary: "Add scheduler client feature to allow scheduled task to run on single/all servers"
description: """Most of the scheduler clients need to be run on a single server only. By default all server in a web farm pickup the scheduled tasks and run them (except in EVOQ where a server is specified).    The scheduler tasks need to be enhanced by allowing them to run on a single server by default and if needed they can be run on multiple server (for example, when flushing a server's local cached objects). This change should be hard-coded in classes that extends the {{SchedulerClient}} and not dynamic so it can't be set for each installation differently.    It appears that {{Purge Log Buffer}} in Platform needs to run on ALL the servers. Others should run on a specific server only.    We need to do two things:  # Add a virtual property *RunsOnSingeServerOnly*  on {{SchedulerClient}} abstract class. The default being true.  # Make a change in Scheduler engine to see if there are more than one server defined in {{WebServers}} table. If so, and we have tasks like {{Search: Site Crawler}} with no servers specified, it should auto-assign that task to itself.   ## While doing this, it must clear cache so other servers can be made aware that a given task has been assigned to another server, and that they should not attempt to run. """
comments: "The other thing that Bruce and I had noticed, is it would be nice if the schedule knew if the site was still starting up or if it was running, and not start any tasks while the site is trying to start. " [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> it's already present. In the Scheduler area there is a setting {{Schedule Start Delay (mins)}}. You need to click on the pencil icon next to {{Scheduler Mode}}." [SEP] "Its very important to understand how the current Web Server Monitor task works with this. When servers go offline, the Web Server Monitor sets them to 'disabled' in the web servers table AND finds any scheduled tasks where the server is named as the 'run on' server, and chooses a new server to allocate that task to.    That behaviour works when the server is assigned correctly.  Where we have a problem is when the server goes offline and is not detected that that happened, and then the web server

summary: "Reduce the spent time of the InstallWizard main request"
description: """It is known that some environments throws exceptions when a request is slow. So after a deep investigation, we have found that the InstallWizard main request could run into that problem since it is alive during all the install process.    This improvement pretends to reduce the time of that request. As far as we have found, the appropriate approach could be to split the request into smaller ones dealing with a unique installation step."""

Thks" [SEP] "Hi [~accountid:<USER>， im not add this attribute new, the attribute was add as class attribute before, which also contains Get methods, so i move this to each post method in it." summary: "TabModule Pane Doesn't Update on Skin Change"
description: """When a skin has a pane that is not the default required ContentPane, it can cause problems for module ordering when the skin on the page is changed.    To reproduce this, add a new module to a page, and put it in a custom pane, example TopHeroDark pane.    Then change the skin on the page to a skin which does not have the TopHeroDark pane.    The module will be located in the ContentPane on the page, but if you look in the database, it still thinks it is in the TopHeroDark pane in the TabModules table.    This can cause problems with module ordering and drag & drop, when you have multiple modules on the page that were in different panes, and they all get lumped into the ContentPane with duplicate ModuleOrder values."""
comments: "this is by design - if you decide to re-apply original skin again afterwards or modify the skin adding a pane with the original name, it will be returned properly.   What might need to be reviewed is order of modules in content pane, I suggest ordering it by pane name and position in pane instead just by position without regarding pane name. "

summary: "Switch from HTTP Module to OWIN hosting"
description: """It will be better to use OWIN so that we can take advantage of available middleware (JWT auth for example) instead of implementing in app layer"""
comments: "I found that using OWIN was pretty straightforward and requires minimal setup on the DNN side. As with a HTTP Handler, it's necessary to have a AUM exclusion [added to the host settings|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Prototype.LiquidContent/blob/DNN-10451-spike-arch/src/Dnn.Module.LiquidContent/Providers/DataProviders/SqlDataProvider/00.00.01.SqlDataProvider#L3] but other than that it just work out of the box.    Problems will arise though when there would be another extension trying to use OWIN for hosting because there can be only one startup class. In the future the way to go would be to have an OWIN startup within DNN Platform and expose a way for extensions to [register in the pipeline|https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Prototype.LiquidContent/blob/DNN-10451-spike-arch/src/Dnn.Module.LiquidContent/Startup.cs#L11]. This is also inevitable path for the prospect of moving to .NET Core eventually"

summary: "Change default robots.txt to allow client side resources"
description: """+Background+  Google has changed the way that it crawls pages, so that it can properly render and understand the content in a page.    Part of this is accessing the js, css and image files that go into many webpages, particularly if they use a delayed load.    As per the [official guidelines from Google Webmaster tools|http://googlewebmastercentral.blogspot.ro/2014/10/updating-our-technical-webmaster.html]:  {quote}  For optimal rendering and indexing, our new guideline specifies that you should allow Googlebot access to the JavaScript, CSS, and image files that your pages use. This provides you optimal rendering and indexing for your site. Disallowing crawling of Javascript or CSS files in your site’s robots.txt directly harms how well our algorithms render and index your content and can result in suboptimal rankings.  {quote}    The existing standard robots.txt file by default blocks access to specific DNN/CSS paths.    +Change+  * *Remove* the following lines from the googlebot section of the robots.txt file:      This is a simple change which will help with Googlebot crawling Dnn sites still using the default robots.txt file.  """
comments: "Is this change going to be retroactive (i.e. will it update existing {{robots.txt}} files)?  Also, {{DesktopModules}} and {{Resources}} need to be unblocked as well, since they have CSS and JS in them.    Is there value in the other exclusions that the file presently has?  I can see someone (though not everyone) wanting to avoid blocking the user account pages, I guess, but what are we trying to solve by disallowing access to these other paths?" [SEP] "Brian - good point on the DesktopModules folder - perhaps that and the Resources folder as well.  I don't think there is any risk in allowing indexing of those folders.    The user profiles were excluded to remove the 'payload' for registration spam.  By excluding indexing on user profile pages, any spam postings to those user accounts is not going to be indexed." [SEP] "I somehow missed the comments regarding DesktopModules and Resources prior to submitting pull request #957.  If it needs to be updated to remove these two, just let me know." summary: "Remove the revision part from built assemblies"
description: """In DNN products every new build has a new assembly version with the 'revision' part set to the build number.    I suggest removing the 'revision' part from the {{AssemblyVersion}}, but keep it in the {{AssemblyFileVersion}}. This makes testing with various DLL's much easier during the development time.    For example, I can drop {{DotNetNuke.dll}} file from a newer build into my local installation without having to replace all the DLL's that depend on that specific previous build of this file. Therefore, we avoid the YSOD errors of ASP.NET in such cases during testing a small change in the Platform main library without the need to create a complete build of the whole site.      The same can be applied to all built DLL files."""

Suppose you want to create an Exception Filter that redirects the user to a special error page.      In the case above whenever there is an exception the action should automatically redirect to an ErrorPage() which is a method on the controller that returns a type of `ActionResult`.    This is something I can help solve but I may need someone that I could message that has a little bit more domain knowledge of the MVC Architecture."""

This currently works with an `ActionFilter` that implements `IExceptionFilter`.    I am going to continue to work through this and try to get the other `ActionFilters` to properly redirect and then I'll submit a Pull Request" [SEP] "I believe this should be changed to Feature Request instead of bug since this is a feature that appears to have not been implemented. summary: "SI: Ability to locate Orphaned Files"
description: """A customer would like the ability to locate Orphaned files (Old files that aren't being used on pages, modules, etc) and way to remove them once found.    This would be useful for website clean-up and making it easy to free up space on the site.    Additional Comments from customer:    Document Scenario – A new version of an existing pdf or other file type is uploaded to the website to replace an existing document. The content editor doesn’t take the care to use the exact same file name and overwrite the existing pdf. Instead the new file with some unique file name is uploaded and linked to in the page content and the old file remains, no longer linked to or in use. The old file doesn’t get deleted from the site and has been orphaned from use on any page.    Image scenario – Images are frequently updated, each with its own unique file name. Previously viewed old images simply pile up in their folders and become clutter.    Desired functionality would identify files in the /Document and /Image directories that have no active relationship with any page and provide admin the ability to manage them individually or in mass.    Additional benefit: remove files that search engines may otherwise provide inappropriate search results to."""
comments: "There is no way for us to take a full inventory and define what isn't in use (there are third party modules to consider, plus people providing direct links); therefore I am closing this as won't fix because it isn't possible. " [SEP] "I will also add that we are adding some analytics and tracking for assets in 9.2 which could help in the future but won't be a 100% solution as they described here. "

summary: "Remove outdated Navigation Providers"
description: """DNN Platform contains several Navigation providers which are outdated and no longer supported.  These providers add to the overall memory footprint of the platform and slow down the startup time for the platform.  As a result the following projects and assemblies should be removed:  * ASP2MenuNavigationProviders  * DNNDropDownNavigationProvider  * DNNTreeNavigationProvider  * SolpartMenuNavigationProviders  * SolpartWebControls    *NOTE:* This is a *breaking change*.  Old skins which rely on these menus will break.  We have removed all references to Solpart Menu and any skins or containers which used them are not compatible with DNN 8.  This is by design."""
comments: "AFAIK those providers are no longer installed by default (in all versions of DNN 7)" [SEP] "Merged PR https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/724" [SEP] "[~accountid:<USER> Unfortunately, those providers are still installed by default into the \bin\providers directory.  Originally, we created the \providers directory so that assemblies wouldn't get loaded automatically at startup, but were available if called. This worked well until we added WebAPI support, and subsequently started using MEF.  Both of those features scan the entire folder tree under the \bin folder looking for assemblies/classes which implement certain interfaces.  This causes any assembly in the \bin or child folders to get loaded into memory, even if they will never be used.  As a result we have been loading a lot of unneeded assemblies into memory for the past four or five years without realizing it.  This enhancement is part of the cleanup we are doing in DNN 8 to get rid of unused features which add bloat to DNN and cause perfo

summary: "SI: File moving not transactional, doesn't rollback from error"
description: """Please see linked issue.    """"[...] we moved one our top level folders, it has numerous sub folders that probably go 4 or 5 levels deep. The end user wanted to change the name of the top level folder from """"Planning"""" to """"Planning & Building"""".     The end user used the move function and at some point she received an error stating that the folder couldn't be created because the length of the path was too long. I believe this is a windows limitation.     The underlying issue is really that the process is not transactional, meaning that if it fails it doesn't [roll] back. It started the process of moving files and folders and when it encountered the error it just halted. Leaving the system in a state where most of the files had been moved but not all.     Further my guess is that the system is updated to look at the new folders that were created from the move when the process completes successfully and because it did not complete successfully the system was not aware of the new folders hence to the end user it looked like the files were gone""""    This can be worked around by re-syncing the folders, but the client would like the errors to be handled more gracefully"""

summary: "Creating new user creates many empty profile entries "
description: """When a new user is created there are 23 profile rows created in the {{UserProfile}} table with most of them having empty {{PropertyValue}}. This is a waste of database space and inefficient in terms of database operations and needs to be optimized by not creating the rows with empty values in the first place.    A further enhancement is to delete these already existing rows with empty values."""
comments: "We need to be very carefully handling this, due to DNN using an empty string internally as Null value.  With profile properties, we need to distinguish between a non-set value (in this case, we may use the default) or an intentionally empty string. Consider default for a custom property Profession is ""enter your profession"", a user might empty this, to indicate he doesn't want his profession being stored.  The code behind might decide, whether the text has not been provided or is intentionally empty.  Besides, it is usually easier to handle records with missing values than deciding whether to use the value, an empty string or the default value, especially in the database. Be also aware that inner joins usually perform faster than left joins.  If we want to optimize user profile, I would alter PropertyValue to nVarChar and remove PropertyText column." [SEP] "https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1605  https://github.com/dnnsoftware/Dnn.Platform/pull/1606" [SEP] "PR merged, change is available in

summary: "WEB API performance investigation"
description: """During profiling Platform performance, I found that DNN's WEB API requests take 1 to 2 milli-seconds more than the time taken by those requests from a default WEB API application created by VS. We need to investigate the cause of this extra time which could be coming from authentication/authorization, extra filters, AUM, etc.) and investigate whether there is a possibility to reduce/remove these extra milli-seconds.    Note: the test was done against a """"do-nothing"""" DNN WEB API method against a default WEB API application method created by VS 2015 project which returns a single string value. The average time taken by DNN app was about 3 to 4 ms while the default app was 2 to 3 ms.    I attached a JMeter image of performance comparison between the two calls ( !WebApiPerf.png|thumbnail! )"""
comments: "I’ve investigated the WEB API performance in DNN and found few sources for the performance hit we incur. Here are the major identified areas:  - The rewrite and process request methods of the {{AdvancedUrlRewriter}} are other areas. This is the most costly area in performance for WEB API calls.      - Injected {{DnnContextMessageHandler}} class in the API processing pipeline. This one is called in each API request to setup the proper context for the call. The major time is consumed in creating the portal setting object in this call:    In this call the  is called which is the one responsible about consuming most of the time.    Measuring these with JMeter against a plain vanilla WEB API service created as a Visual Studio C# project, showed that these are adding more than 1 millisecond on local machine when there are no Database access and all values are obtained from the cache.    To improve this and reduce some of the extra overhead would require a huge amount of work to reduce the re-writer logic and cre

comments: "+The logic that we are adopting in the needed fixes:+    *Prompt* is a feature the allow command (cmd) line access for the application's functionality.  - Thus what you see in the prompt cmd *result *should match what you get from the application normal functionalities done using the the UI.    *Prompt cross portal access* is a way to execute the usual prompt commands but with targeting a site other than the one in the URL, for e.g. ""Mainsite/API"".  - Thus the *result *will be similar to executing the prompt command on that remote targeted site.  - Targeting a site to execute commands on is done by adding ""/siteId"" at the end of the API call, for e.g. : ""Mainsite/API/ 2"".    *Site-Group* is a feature in DNN that allows specific sites ,which exist in a specific site group, to share users & modules.  - This should affect the *result * of running commands inside the *basic prompt* to extend the allowed accessible items to be retrieved or manipulated only for features affected by siteGroups (Users & module

summary: "Use DateTime.UtcNow instead of DateTime.Now"
description: """Throughout the DNN code, there's a lot of incorrect uses of DateTime.Now to store values into the database. DateTime.Now is the representation of the configured timezone on the server (EST, CST, etc.). We should always be storing DateTime.UtcNow into the database so that values can be converted back to any local time, as needed. It makes DNN more portable when switching hosting providers in different geographical data centers because UTC datetime is always the same regardless of timezone or daylight saving. To be truly enterprise ready and secure, DNN needs a consistent way to store date and time.    There is also a performance benefit to switch, because DateTime.UtcNow is many times faster than DateTime.Now. The .NET implementation of DateTime.Now is actually taking DateTime.UtcNow and converting it to local time of the server so it's doing a lot of unnecessary arithmetic.    [https://blogs.msdn.microsoft.com/kirillosenkov/2012/01/10/datetime-utcnow-is-generally-preferable-to-datetime-now/]  [https://dzone.com/articles/darkness-behind-datetimenow]  """
comments: "Hi Stephen, this sounds like a good addition.  However, we are not able to get any additional items into the release unless it has a major impact on usability.  I'd like to address this in future releases.  "

summary: "Implement Trace Statements in Dnn Platform Code"
description: """+Background+    It can be difficult to do performance troubleshooting on a live running Dnn site, if some specific module is not performing well.    This change is to add in trace output statements to the codebase, so that anyone can hook up any trace listener (such as Dnn Glimpse) and determine which components on a page are running slowly.    As the Log4Net component provides a trace listener, it can be leveraged for this purpose and have the output directed via the log4net configuration.    +Requirements+  * Implement the trace statements in code as provided  * Add the required log4Net configuration change to this JIRA as an example  * Test by checking with a trace listener such as Glimpse"""
comments: "In order to use Log4Net to trace these particular messages in {{System.Diagnostics.Trace}}, this is what we have to set up in the file {{DotNetNuke.log4net.config}}:  * First, there will be another appender for this type of traces:  {code:xml}  <appender name=""TraceAppender"" type=""log4net.Appender.TraceAppender"">      <layout type=""log4net.Layout.PatternLayout"">          <conversionPattern value=""%date - %message%newline"" />      </layout>  </appender>  {code}  * Second, we have to add a Logger, to log those particular traces:  {code:xml}  <logger name=""DNN.Trace"" additivity=""false"">      <level value=""DEBUG"" />      <appender-ref ref=""TraceAppender"" />  </logger>  {code}    With these changes, if we want to send traces to the {{System.Diagnostics.Trace}} environment by using log4net, this is what we have to do:  {code:java}  DnnLogger.GetLogger(""DNN.Trace"").Debug(""This is a test"");  {code}    The primary purpose of these trace statements is to collect data on performance when loading pag

summary: "Installing modules very slow with many portals"
description: """The module install page (/Install/Install.aspx?mode=installresources) is very slow on sites with a lot of portals due to a high number of database calls.  That process currently calls GetPortalDesktopModules for every module on every portal.    I've implemented a fix which only calls GetPortalDesktopModules once per module in the outer loop and then passes that dictionary of modules down to the inner loop.  The new dictionary parameter is optional, and if the parameter is not provided it will fall back on the old mechanism (fetching from the database).  This maintains backwards compatibility and isolates the changes to the module install page.    Our DNN instance has 35 modules and over 3600 portals.  We did a database trace and found that this was making over 100,000 database calls.  This was causing our install process to take over 31 minutes with no load (about an hour with load).    After implementing the change, we have significantly reduced our install time from 31 minutes to under 1.5 minutes (from 58 to under 4 minutes with load).    The patch file for this fix is attached, I have discussed it with [~accountid:<USER> and will submit a pull request shortly."""

summary: "DCC API - SQL Caching"
description: """Ideally SQL queries responsible for read Dynamic Content entities (Content Type, Content Items, Data Type, Templates) should be always cached to obtain great performance.    Cache should be defined at first at the API level, so inside the manager classes that expose the DCC functionality to internal or third party modules.    Caching is currently implemented for all the managers using AOP (see Cacheable Annotation). See example below:      However, the *DynamicContentItemManager* use internally the ContentController that does not cache the method *GetContentItemsByModuleId*.  We must cache this call as currently is used in the part more sensible to performance that is the DCC Viewer (see below):   !DCCViewerSQLCallForAnonymous.png!    *Important*: we likely will change the approach to retrieve the content item associated to a module as commented in the jira so please review this jira once DNN-7796 is done.    """

summary: "Removal of layout mode limits support functionality of the platform"
description: """Removal of the layout mode from a regular user perspective appears to be an overall net """"Gain"""", however, there was one major customer support aspect of the functionality that no longer exists in the platform.    In prior versions of DNN you could enable Layout Mode and then navigate to a page with a module that was throwing exceptions and remove the offending module.  Now, if you have a module that causes certain kinds of exceptions that break rendering of the page your options for removal are more limited.    Is there any possiblity of adding/retaining a functionality to support this?"""

I could see possibly adding a query string parameter option, but I would prefer not to see this introduced in the UI because it has very limited value.    -Will" [SEP] "Will -     I figured that was going to be the argument here, but I wanted to try as there is benefit in environments with un-named modules etc where the placement of the module is helpful to know.    Example Situation:    Page with 15 modules on it, none of those modules have titles to them.  You add a new module with a blank title, it is at the bottom of the Content Pane, and it blows up entirely.    In this case the Modules list in the page settings doesn't help as I cannot target it directly.      A querystring personally, that was a hidden support feature would be awesome.  As I know the framework underneath still supports it." [SEP] "We will add the ability to access layout mode via query string in the next release.    -Will"

summary: "SI: Module Settings - Publish Date: Allow Roles with Edit Rights to view module"
description: """Currently, if a specific role has access to view/edit/manage settings for a module then the role would be able to see the module during its publication date. Nevertheless, if the Start date is in the future or if the end date has already passed (in other words if the module is no longer published), then the module would not be visible for the role with edits right and will not be able to work on it.    The use case where this feature is needed is when one has a custom role managing important information/announcement banners which are not always visible; and the users in this role should have the rights to edit and publish these modules. However, once the publish date does not match the ranges between start and end dates, users in that role are no longer able to view nor edit the module."""

summary: "Profanity filter definition issues"
description: """The following problems have been detected trying to define profanity filters.    By default, this filter is defined in a clean install:  Text=FindThisText, Value=ReplaceWithNothing    Note that Text can have duplicates, but Value can't.    In the case we would need to define filters so several prohibited words change to """"censored"""", we would need to define:  - Text=ProhibitedWord1, Value=censored  - Text=ProhibitedWord2, Value=censored  This is not possible, as value can't have duplicates    In the other hand, it is possible to create other filters like:  - Text=SomeProhibitedWord, Value=aaaa  - Text=SomeProhibitedWord, Value=bbbb  In this case, it is not well defined to which value, the appareances of 'SomeProhibitedWord' will be replaced to.    Summarizing, for profanity, the duplicate check would need to be done for Text, not for Value."""

summary: "SI: CKEditor cursor location on switch to source view"
description: """While editing content with the CKEditor, if the user highlights text then switches to source view, they lose their place in the content. In the basic editor switching to HTML view takes you to the location of text you have highlighted in design view. A client is accustomed to this functionality and is saying how hard it is to find their place in the HTML source.    Here are the steps to reproduce:    1. Go to a page with an HTML module that has a significant amount of content.    2. Put the page in edit mode.    3. Click on the module content to enter simple edit mode. Don’t go into advanced edit mode yet.    4. In the basic editor, scroll down a bit to some content in the middle of the module and highlight some text.    5. Click the toolbar button to go into the HTML source code (still in the basic editor).    6. Note that the text you highlighted in design view is now still highlighted in source code view.    7. Click the toolbar button to go back to design view.    8. Go into advanced edit (CKEditor) mode.    9. Repeat steps 4 and 5 in the advanced editor.    10. Note when you switch to the HTML source code view, your cursor is at the top of the content and the text you highlighted in design view is not highlighted in HTML source view. To find the code you were looking at, you have to be able to decipher the source code or use the browser’s find function, that is _if_ you can remember what the text was and don’t mis-type anything in the search box."""

summary: "slow performance of GetUserByPasswordResetToken"
description: """On sites with many users (> 100k), the procedure tends to time out, as it scans all records due to a missing index.  The best solution would be a dedicated table PasswordResetTokens.  As an easier fix, I suggest adding a filtered index on PasswordResetToken  of users table (where passwordResetToken is not Null) and clearing all outdated and used tokens when possible, which keeps the index very small and fast.  Limitation: the system might no longer be able to identify and display, whether a token is just outdated or (not issues or already been used) - but IMO this is not necessary.    I volunteer to provide necessary SQL code, if accepted (should not require API changes)"""

summary: "Possibility to reinstate DNN8 View layout and Pane postions"
description: """This is really a list of features that have been removed in DNN9 and I think they are a backwards step.  1. No view layout mode.  this was good because you could visualize the panes  in a skin and then demonstrate to non techy users exactly where they should place new content .Also  a quick way to see where page modules are positioned.    2.The removal of pane names in edit mode is not helpful to non technical administrators, they now have no real idea where they should drag and drop a new module.      As all the panes are collapsed with no descriptions???   trying to figure what pane or row of panes an existing module is using is tricky to say the least, for example it is not obvious whether the pane you choose is right/left narrow or left/right wide or which row it belongs too.    3. When adding a new module I can no longer specify the correct pane in advance. I have to place it then right click to choose a pane. Ok,  in theory but I refer to the previous question.    4. If I bring in an existing module from another page it totally screws up the positions of other modules on the page and I have to reset them all again (at least it does on my package).    I  have to write a training module for our 200+ """"non technical"""" editors and the section on how to add new modules to a specified particular pane is going to be a pain in the *@#%.  And copying an existing module is likely to be very confusing, even too much to get their heads around.    One of the selling points for DNN for me is its really easy interface, I appreciate that liquid content is the way forward, but many orgs like mine (local government)   where we have lots of editors who's only digital experience is word or excel, so the lack of easy to visualize placement is detrimental """

comments: "This was from an email from [~accountid:<USER>:  ""I was reviewing the issue. The issue is related to the HTML module settings. I was able to reproduce it and the problematic setting is ‘HtmlText_UseDecorate’. The value for this setting in 7.3.4 is ""1"" but this value isn’t casted properly in 8.0.0, so we get these errors. In 8.0.0 this boolean settings are stored as “True"" or “False"". As workaround the user can set this setting to “True” in the modules that they are having problems. """ [SEP] "The problem was related to how the new module settings repository works. In Platform 7.3.4 some boolean module settings were stored in database as ""1"" or ""0"". With the new implementation, it only allows to parse string as boolean if their value is ""True"" or ""False"", otherwise, it is not able to convert the value into a boolean.     In this scenario, the HTML module had a boolean setting ""HtmlText_UseDecorate"" that in some scenarios had set. Since all the settings for a module is retrieved at

summary: "Geography DataTypes"
description: """Not sure if this has been discussed but adding support for DataTypes SQL Geography and SQL Geometry would be amazing for the DCC"""
comments: "The DCC is not built around SQL data types.  Supporting these as native data types would require a significant re-architecting of the DCC."

summary: "Extract User Manager into a separate module"
description: """h2. Card The User Manager is used for managing portal and host users for a site. The User Manager is just a UI that exists over the top of the core platform APIs.  The UI components should be extracted into a separate module, however the underlying public API and data components should remain part of the platform.  h2. Conversation This initial conversion effort is only focused on conversion to a module and shall not include conversion to a MVC or SPA module.  The new user management module should manage both portal users and host users.  Host users should only be visible and manageable by other host users.  Once installed, the user manager will only exist in the Admin menu.  The host menu user management page should be removed on upgrades.   h2. Confirmation """
comments: "After discussion with Joe/Charles, this item will be marked as ""wont fix"". We will tackle this at a later point when we are rewriting the user management area and can better separate concerns.Note: I attached a document I generated with some detail to help, and that demonstrated the limited scope of extraction"

summary: "Social Providers - Add ability to select fields/data to be passed to FB, LI, etc where applicable"
description: """Found in the comments of DNN-7446    Hey guys!    Before you get this ready for DNN 8.0, please add the following MUCH needed functionality:    1) In the FB Authentication Provider settings (or in any of the social network authentication provider settings), you should be able to select which facebook fields you want to retrieve, and then map them to normal and custom profile fields for the user. If possible this should include address, phone number.    2) You should have the ability to retrieve the facebook profile picture, and then import this for use as the User's avatar. If you can get this to work for the other providers that would be cool too.    I understand we aren't going to get a hotfix for this type of enhancement, but it should be included in the next major release of DNN. Please let us know if you are going to be able to do this.    Thanks,    <USER> """
comments: "These providers need to be re-written as connectors (the underlying provider can be updated, just add a new UI) and this is not slated to happen in the near future."

summary: "I/E: Site Export"
description: """As a Host or Admin user, I want the ability to select portions of my site or my whole site, and export that as a .zip package for use in any other DNN site--for the purpose of creating new sites or moving from staging to production site. As much as we can, on behalf of the exporting user, we want to include all dependencies they will need for the content they are exporting (extension packages, for example). This functionality will be exposed in a new menu item under the Persona Bar > Settings area. Host users will also be able to get to this area via the Sites area within the persona bar (to export a specific site).     As a user selects options and then progress through the export process, they need to be presented with visual feedback letting them know what is occurring as they progress; including display of any warnings and errors identified as part of the export process.     *PERMISSION*  Admin and Host user have access to this functionality. The Admin user will have the ability to export the contents of only their site; whereas the Host user will be able to export any site.    *WHAT ARE WE EXPORTING*  Existing:  * Content - HTML, Content Types, Content Items and Visualizers  * Files  * Roles  * Profile Properties  * Pages (pick list with option to select/de-select all with a single click)    New:  * Users (each user's membership information: username, password, email, )  * * User profile property values (if users are selected AND profile properties)  * Extension packages (No UI)  ** Modules/Providers/Themes/Language Packs  ** Folder permissions  * HTML Provider Configuration (No UI)  * Vocabularies  * URL's and redirects (No UI)  ** Any AUM settings (site level), any page level redirects  * Evoq page templates (No UI)  * Evoq Connector Data (most of this is probably under portal settings)  * All Portal Settings data (any property from the table should automatically)  ** Anything related to portal alias data will not be included  * All Page Settings not currently included, per page  ** This should include any page header, scripts, etc. seen via the page settings UI    *ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA*  Upon Export, a .zip package should be created that installs into any other like-versioned DNN site (also log the export in the admin logs under a new log type for export). Import will be handled in a separate story.     FEEDBACK FROM SPRINT REVIEW:  * Remove field names (Template Name becomes Name)  * No difference between Platform and Evoq modules - take out the home page thumbnail  * Needs to be a warning in the UI when exporting *encrypted passwords* because they likely won't come with.  *Future Enhancement: restrict export job to one server."""
